source.attribution: "Natural Korean Conversation with 태웅쌤 | 이렇게 귀하신 분이 ①" by Didi's Korean Culture Podcast, used under Creative Commons Attribution license (reuse allowed).

units.english_guidance: There are a few people, and/but… Preserve the unfinished continuation and the respectful people/counter reading of 분.

facts: The phrase refers to multiple people.
The utterance is incomplete and continues beyond this window.
catastrophic_if: Treats 분 as minutes rather than people.

units.english_guidance: A brief affirmative or attentive backchannel such as ‘yes,’ ‘right,’ or ‘okay.’

facts: The speaker gives a brief affirmative or attentive acknowledgment.

units.korean_gold: 어떤 분은 태국 갔다 오셨다고 했거든요. 근데 태국이 진짜 좋았다고 그러시더라구요.
units.english_guidance: One person said they had been to Thailand, and said that Thailand was really good. Natural variants may render the honorific and reported-speech framing differently.

facts: A person is reported to have visited Thailand.
That person is reported to have found Thailand very good.
catastrophic_if: Reverses the report into not visiting Thailand or disliking Thailand.

units.english_guidance: A brief affirmative or attentive backchannel such as ‘yes,’ ‘right,’ or ‘okay.’

facts: The speaker gives a brief affirmative or attentive acknowledgment.

units.english_guidance: ‘Why? Why?’ Preserve that the question is repeated.

facts: The speaker asks why twice in immediate repetition.

units.korean_gold: 아니 그 예전에 코로나 사태 이전에 누나랑
units.english_guidance: ‘No/well, back before the COVID crisis, with my older sister…’ The next window confirms that 누나 is the male speaker’s biological older sister; this phrase remains unfinished.

facts: The time is before the COVID crisis.
The speaker refers to being with his older sister.
The phrase is incomplete at the window boundary.
catastrophic_if: Changes the older-sister relationship to a spouse, younger sibling, or unrelated person.
Places the event after COVID rather than before it.

units.english_guidance: A brief affirmative or attentive backchannel such as ‘yes,’ ‘right,’ or ‘okay.’

facts: The speaker gives a brief affirmative or attentive acknowledgment.

units.english_guidance: ‘My biological older sister…’ The subject-marked fragment continues into the following speech.

facts: The referent is the male speaker’s biological older sister.
The subject-marked phrase is incomplete.
catastrophic_if: Changes 친누나 to a younger sister, spouse, or unrelated woman.

units.english_guidance: A brief affirmative or attentive backchannel such as ‘yes,’ ‘right,’ or ‘okay.’

facts: The speaker gives a brief affirmative or attentive acknowledgment.

units.korean_gold: 그 막 그 매형이랑 태국에 한 달 살기를 하려고 했는데
units.english_guidance: With her husband—the speaker’s brother-in-law—his older sister had planned to live in Thailand for a month, but… Preserve that this was an intention and that the clause is unfinished.

facts: 매형 refers to the male speaker’s older sister’s husband.
The plan was to live in Thailand for one month.
The construction expresses an intention and ends with an unfinished contrast or explanation.
catastrophic_if: Misidentifies 매형 as the speaker’s own husband or as a different family relationship.
Turns the one-month Thailand plan into a completed stay in another place or for another duration.

units.korean_gold: 그 내용이 너무 바쁘셔가지고 남는 사람이
units.english_guidance: The audio-grounded wording is transcribed as 그 내용이; it says the referent was very busy and leads into ‘the person left/available…’. Keep the referent unresolved and the phrase incomplete.

facts: A referent is described as very busy.
The phrase leads into identifying the person remaining or available and is incomplete.

units.english_guidance: A brief affirmative or attentive backchannel such as ‘yes,’ ‘right,’ or ‘okay.’

facts: The speaker gives a brief affirmative or attentive acknowledgment.

units.korean_gold: 저밖에 없어서 그 공짜로 태국 한 달 살기를 한 적이 있어요 아 근데 그 치앙마이에서 한 달을 살았는데 너무 동네가 예쁘고 사람들도 너무 친절하고 한국어
units.english_guidance: Because I was the only one available, I once got to live in Thailand for a month for free. I lived in Chiang Mai for a month; the neighborhood was very pretty and the people were very kind, and then the clip cuts off on ‘Korean…’. Preserve both one-month references and the unfinished ending.

facts: The speaker was the only person remaining or available.
The speaker once lived in Thailand for one month for free.
The speaker lived in Chiang Mai for one month.
The neighborhood was very pretty and the people were very kind.
The utterance cuts off after beginning a phrase with 한국어.
catastrophic_if: Changes the month-long Thailand and Chiang Mai stay to a different country, city, or duration.
Reverses the free stay into a paid stay or the kind people into hostile people.

notes: Audio-grounded via bench/MOM_LISTEN_PASS.md; informal unblinded listen (saw ‘Computer heard’); candidate only; NOT frozen; NOT dual-reviewed; NOT adjudicated; NOT scoreable.
